scope.goal: Audit whether the semantic-kernel vocabulary is organized around reusable capabilities rather than language or API feature labels.
non_goals: Do not add public semantic-kernel APIs.
Do not open new exact semantic admissions.
Do not rewrite checked historical artifacts in place.

count_derivation.evidence_and_source_enums: Extracted from current Rust enum variants in crates/nose-il/src/node/evidence.rs and crates/nose-il/src/node/source.rs.
count_derivation.runtime_boundary_rules: Parsed from RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_OBLIGATIONS in crates/nose-cli/src/recall_loss_report/obligations.rs, including runtime_rule! entries and explicit RuntimeBoundaryRule entries.

accepted_capability_groups.decision: Source facts preserve syntax and protocol distinctions erased by the shared IL; exact consumers must still require downstream contracts before admission.
Domain evidence and DomainRequirement stay as reusable receiver/result constraints, not selector-name proof.
Import and symbol evidence are identity/provenance capabilities that other packs compose with; they do not approve semantics by themselves.
CallTarget evidence remains a target-identity capability for direct, method, imported, and dynamic-dispatch distinctions.
LibraryApi evidence records an occurrence of a known selector or coordinate; exact admission still requires receiver, domain, arity, shadowing, demand, and effect preconditions.

PromiseSettledValue is accepted as the current settled-value channel evidence. The name is JS-oriented, so future non-JS FutureLike settled-value work should group it under the shared success/error result-channel capability rather than create a parallel feature list.
SequenceSurface keeps exact-safe sequence materialization separate from raw syntax and from library API selectors.
DemandEffectProfile is the shared capability for eager, lazy, callback, short-circuit, async, generator, and channel-like execution obligations.
Place and Effect evidence should remain fact-level mutation/effect capabilities that block unsafe exact convergence until dependencies are closed.
Runtime-boundary family/subreason reporting is the right abstraction layer for recall-loss diagnostics. Subreasons may retain selector-shaped labels only when they identify the missing proof.
Scheduling, lifecycle, materialization, cancellation, and channel obligations should stay protocol capabilities shared by JS/TS, Python, Rust, Go, Java, Swift, and Ruby.
Result, exception, and rejection channels are accepted as channel capabilities; language-specific spellings must map onto them instead of creating independent exact paths.
Callback identity, demand, result, and effect obligations stay as reusable exact-channel preconditions across HOFs, executors, goroutines, defers, promises, and futures.
Pack descriptors and conformance checks are the extension boundary; #654 found no need to expand that public API for this audit.
LawPack/value-graph semantics remain separate from evidence emission and exact admission; no feature-shaped drift found in this audit slice.

legacy_aliases.decision: Preserve readability for historical checked artifacts; prefer the canonical shared label in new reports and docs.
Preserve readability for historical checked artifacts; prefer the canonical shared label in new reports and docs.
Preserve readability for historical checked artifacts; prefer the canonical shared label in new reports and docs.

legacy_aliases.alias: EvidenceEmitter::FirstParty Rust associated const and Builtin serde alias
legacy_aliases.decision: Keep Rust source compatibility and current wire compatibility distinct: the Builtin variant still serializes as "FirstParty" and accepts "Builtin" as a deserialization alias. No public wire-label migration is proposed by this audit.

feature_shaped_entries_requiring_follow_.decision: Keep as a precise missing-proof diagnostic now; #656 should document or normalize the capability grouping.
Keep because JS/TS Promise producer recovery currently needs this proof; #656 should clarify its relationship to async-function scheduling.
Keep separate from method/imported variants only because the producer proof source differs.
Keep separate from direct-function/imported variants only because the producer proof source differs.
Keep as imported settled-value contract proof; document under settled-value channel capability rather than imported-function feature support.
Keep as imported settled-value contract proof; document under settled-value channel capability rather than imported-member feature support.
Keep as receiver proof for the FutureLike continuation capability; avoid treating .then as selector-only admission.
Keep as a producer-channel diagnostic; future non-JS FutureLike factories should map to the shared channel vocabulary.

duplicate_or_merge_candidates.decision: Treat as a legacy alias pair, not two capabilities.
Treat as a legacy alias pair, not two capabilities.
Treat as a legacy alias pair, not two capabilities.
Keep as separate diagnostics, but document both under call-target producer proof.
Keep as separate diagnostics, but document both under imported settled-value channel proof.
Keep channel-specific subreasons, but group them as continuation channel obligations in docs and follow-up planning.

follow_up_decisions.decision: Use this audit as the input for vocabulary/documentation cleanup: prefer canonical shared labels in new docs, keep historical aliases readable, and clarify feature-shaped subreasons as missing-proof diagnostics.
Use the audited capability groups when reporting oracle exclusions so strict fail-closed behavior is not confused with missing observability or unsupported selector features.
Any later behavior-changing async/scheduling exact slice must cite the capability group it opens, add adjacent hard negatives, and record semantic admission/performance deltas.

validation_commands: jq -e '.summary.capability_groups_audited == (.accepted_capability_groups | length) and .summary.evidence_record_kinds_audited == (.audited_code_vocabularies.evidence_kind | length) and .summary.source_fact_kinds_audited == (.audited_code_vocabularies.source_fact_kind | length) and .summary.source_protocol_kinds_audited == (.audited_code_vocabularies.source_protocol_kind | length) and .summary.runtime_boundary_rules_audited == .count_derivation.runtime_boundary_rule_breakdown.runtime_rules_total and .summary.promise_prefixed_runtime_obligation_labels ==
